SignalR benchmark tool

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Show version information

Commands:

Run 'dotnet signalr-bench [command] --help' for more information about a command.

dotnet-signalr-bench 1.0.0-preview1-10415

SignalR benchmark tool

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench [options] [command]

Options:
  --version    Show version information
  --help       Show help information

Commands:
  agent        Agent command options
  application  Application command options
  controller   Controller command options

Run 'dotnet signalr-bench [command] --help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
agent

Agent command options

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench agent [options]

Options:
  --help        Show help information
  -p|--port     Port to use [7000]. Default is 7000
  -v|--verbose  Show more console output.

result.stdout:
application

Application command options

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench application [options]

Options:
  --help                      Show help information
  -p|--port <PORT>            Port to use [5050]. Use 0 for a dynamic port.
  -q|--quiet                  Show less console output.
  -t|--signalr-type           SignalR type: 0 or 1. 0 for local selfhost SignalR, 1 for Azure SignalR Service, default it is 0.
  -c|--server-connection      Set the server connection count to ASRS, default is 5.
  -s|--connection-string      Specify the ASRS connection string.
  -l|--access-token-lifetime  Specify access token's life time in hour, default is 24 hours.
  -v|--verbose                Show more console output.

commands.payload:
application

Application command options

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench application [options]

Options:
  --help                      Show help information
  -p|--port <PORT>            Port to use [5050]. Use 0 for a dynamic port.
  -q|--quiet                  Show less console output.
  -t|--signalr-type           SignalR type: 0 or 1. 0 for local selfhost SignalR, 1 for Azure SignalR Service, default it is 0.
  -c|--server-connection      Set the server connection count to ASRS, default is 5.
  -s|--connection-string      Specify the ASRS connection string.
  -l|--access-token-lifetime  Specify access token's life time in hour, default is 24 hours.
  -v|--verbose                Show more console output.

result.stdout:
controller

Controller command options

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench controller [options]

Options:
  --help                  Show help information
  -a|--agentlist          Specify the agents endpoint list with ',' as separator. for example, '10.172.1.5:7000,10.172.1.6:7000', Default is 'localhost:7000'
  -c|--configuration      Specify the configuration YAML filename. If it is '?', it will print help for all options of the configuration.
  -w|--webapp             Specify the SignalR web app hub URL. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 'http://localhost:5050/signalrbench'
  -C|--connections        Specify the client connection counts. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 1000.
  -b|--basesending        Specify the base sending counts. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 500.
  -s|--step               Specify the sending number for every step. Typically, the sending message count is gradually increasing to avoid CPU spike. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 500.
  -d|--duration           The duration (milliseconds) of running for a single step. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 240000.
  -S|--scenario           Specify the scenario: echo|broadcast. If you want to try more scenarios, please use --configuration. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is echo.
  -D|--debug              Enable debug for printing more details. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is true.
  -u|--use-builtin-agent  Launch builtin agent for a quick start if you specify 1. It is not recommended for an official perf test. Default is 0.

commands.payload:
controller

Controller command options

Usage: dotnet signalr-bench controller [options]

Options:
  --help                  Show help information
  -a|--agentlist          Specify the agents endpoint list with ',' as separator. for example, '10.172.1.5:7000,10.172.1.6:7000', Default is 'localhost:7000'
  -c|--configuration      Specify the configuration YAML filename. If it is '?', it will print help for all options of the configuration.
  -w|--webapp             Specify the SignalR web app hub URL. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 'http://localhost:5050/signalrbench'
  -C|--connections        Specify the client connection counts. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 1000.
  -b|--basesending        Specify the base sending counts. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 500.
  -s|--step               Specify the sending number for every step. Typically, the sending message count is gradually increasing to avoid CPU spike. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 500.
  -d|--duration           The duration (milliseconds) of running for a single step. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is 240000.
  -S|--scenario           Specify the scenario: echo|broadcast. If you want to try more scenarios, please use --configuration. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is echo.
  -D|--debug              Enable debug for printing more details. This option is ignored if '--configuration' is specified. Default value is true.
  -u|--use-builtin-agent  Launch builtin agent for a quick start if you specify 1. It is not recommended for an official perf test. Default is 0.
